project_name: Aitri Product Graph Visualizer — artifact-cards feature

test_plan.scope: artifact-cards feature — CardManager module (js/cards.js), card styles (css/cards.css), and graph.js tap handler extension
test_plan.out_of_scope: Parent project FRs (FR-001 through FR-012) — covered by parent test suite. Server endpoints — no new endpoints added.

test_levels.unit: node:test — tests CardManager pure functions (renderCard, computePosition, clampToViewport, getCascadeCount, close) using jsdom or lightweight DOM stubs
test_levels.e2e: Playwright — tests full browser interaction: click-to-open, timing (300ms/200ms), pan/zoom coexistence, multi-card DOM state

test_cases.title: Clicking a node opens its artifact card within 300ms
test_cases.given: A graph is rendered with a node of id 'FR-001' visible on screen
test_cases.when: The user clicks the node element with data-id='FR-001'
test_cases.then: A DOM element with class 'artifact-card' and data-node-id='FR-001' exists in document.body within 300ms of the click event, and its textContent contains 'FR-001'

test_cases.title: Clicking an already-open node closes its card (toggle)
test_cases.given: CardManager.openCards contains an entry for nodeId 'FR-001' mapped to an HTMLElement appended to document.body
test_cases.when: CardManager.toggle() is called with a cyNode whose id() returns 'FR-001'
test_cases.then: The HTMLElement for 'FR-001' is removed from document.body and CardManager.openCards.has('FR-001') returns false

test_cases.title: Clicking a parent node opens card AND fires collapse/expand
test_cases.given: A graph with a Feature node 'FR-002' that has 3 visible child User Story nodes
test_cases.when: The user clicks the Feature node 'FR-002'
test_cases.then: A card with data-node-id='FR-002' exists in document.body AND the 3 child nodes are no longer visible in the graph (collapsed) — both effects must be observed

test_cases.title: Card renders all 7 standard fields when all are present
test_cases.given: An artifact object: { id: 'FR-001', title: 'Test FR', description: 'A description', priority: 'MUST', type: 'UX', status: 'approved', acceptance_criteria: ['AC one', 'AC two', 'AC three'], implementation_level: 'functional' }
test_cases.when: CardManager.renderCard(artifact) is called
test_cases.then: The returned HTMLElement contains: textContent including 'FR-001', 'Test FR', 'A description', 'MUST', 'UX', 'functional', and 3 separate <li> elements with textContent 'AC one', 'AC two', 'AC three' respectively

test_cases.title: acceptance_criteria array renders as separate list items, not concatenated
test_cases.given: An artifact object with acceptance_criteria: ['Given A, when B, then C', 'Given D, when E, then F']
test_cases.when: CardManager.renderCard(artifact) is called
test_cases.then: The returned HTMLElement contains exactly 2 <li> elements: one with textContent 'Given A, when B, then C' and one with textContent 'Given D, when E, then F' — not a single element with both strings concatenated

test_cases.title: Card omits description section when description field is absent
test_cases.given: An artifact object: { id: 'TC-001', title: 'A test case', status: 'pending' } (no description field)
test_cases.when: CardManager.renderCard(artifact) is called
test_cases.then:
The returned HTMLElement does not contain any element with textContent 'description' or 'Description' and does not contain an empty

or element with no text

test_cases.title: Status badge for 'approved' uses hex #22c55e background
test_cases.given: An artifact object with status: 'approved'
test_cases.when: CardManager.renderCard(artifact) is called
test_cases.then: The returned HTMLElement contains an element with class 'status-badge' whose computed backgroundColor is rgb(34, 197, 94) (equivalent to #22c55e)

test_cases.title: All 5 status badges use distinct background colors
test_cases.given: 5 artifact objects with statuses: 'pending', 'in_progress', 'approved', 'complete', 'drift'
test_cases.when: CardManager.renderCard() is called for each
test_cases.then: The 5 status badge elements have 5 distinct backgroundColor values: rgb(148, 163, 184), rgb(245, 158, 11), rgb(34, 197, 94), rgb(59, 130, 246), rgb(239, 68, 68) — no two badges share the same color

test_cases.title: Status badge for 'drift' has contrast ratio ≥4.5:1
test_cases.given: STATUS_COLORS.drift = { bg: '#ef4444', text: '#ffffff' }
test_cases.when: Contrast ratio is computed between #ffffff and #ef4444 using WCAG relative luminance formula
test_cases.then: The computed contrast ratio is ≥4.5 (expected: 4.7:1)

test_cases.title: Card anchored at right viewport edge clamps to stay fully visible
test_cases.given: window.innerWidth = 1280, CARD_WIDTH = 320, MARGIN = 8. Node renderedPosition = { x: 1260, y: 100 } (near right edge)
test_cases.when: computePosition({ x: 1260, y: 100 }, 0) and clampToViewport() are called
test_cases.then: The returned left value is ≤ 1280 - 320 - 8 = 952 (card right edge does not exceed innerWidth)

test_cases.title: Card anchored at bottom viewport edge clamps to stay above fold
test_cases.given: window.innerHeight = 800, cardHeight = 400, MARGIN = 8. Node renderedPosition = { x: 100, y: 750 } (near bottom)
test_cases.when: clampToViewport({ left: 116, top: 750 }, cardEl) is called where cardEl.getBoundingClientRect().height = 400
test_cases.then: The returned top value is ≤ 800 - 400 - 8 = 392 (card bottom edge does not exceed innerHeight)

test_cases.title: Card with content taller than 60vh gets scrollable body capped at 60vh
test_cases.given: A graph is loaded and a node with 10 acceptance_criteria items is clicked, producing a card whose natural height would exceed 60% of window.innerHeight (480px on an 800px viewport)
test_cases.when: The card is rendered in the DOM
test_cases.then: The card's .card-body element has a computed max-height of '480px' (60% of 800px) and overflow-y of 'auto' or 'scroll', and the card's total getBoundingClientRect().height is ≤ 480px

test_cases.title: Clicking X removes card from DOM within 200ms
test_cases.given: A card with data-node-id='FR-001' is open in document.body
test_cases.when: The user clicks the button with class 'card-close' inside the card
test_cases.then: Within 200ms, document.querySelector('[data-node-id="FR-001"]') returns null (element not in DOM)

test_cases.title: Closing card A does not remove card B from DOM
test_cases.given: CardManager.openCards has entries for 'FR-001' (cardA) and 'FR-002' (cardB), both appended to document.body
test_cases.when: CardManager.close('FR-001') is called
test_cases.then: document.body contains cardB (data-node-id='FR-002') and does not contain cardA (data-node-id='FR-001'). CardManager.openCards.size === 1 and openCards.has('FR-002') === true

test_cases.title: Graph cy.zoom() value is unchanged after closing a card
test_cases.given: A graph is loaded with cy.zoom() = 1.2, and one card is open
test_cases.when: The user clicks the X button on the open card
test_cases.then: After the card is removed from the DOM, cy.zoom() still returns 1.2 (graph zoom unchanged)

test_cases.title: Clicking 3 distinct nodes opens 3 simultaneous cards
test_cases.given: A graph is rendered with nodes 'FR-001', 'FR-002', 'FR-003' visible
test_cases.when: The user clicks 'FR-001', then 'FR-002', then 'FR-003' in sequence
test_cases.then: document.querySelectorAll('.artifact-card').length === 3, and each card has a distinct data-node-id attribute: 'FR-001', 'FR-002', 'FR-003'

test_cases.title: Scrolling card B does not affect card A's scroll position
test_cases.given: Two cards are open: card A (data-node-id='FR-001') and card B (data-node-id='FR-002'), both with scrollable bodies. Card A's .card-body.scrollTop = 0
test_cases.when: The user scrolls card B's body element by 50px
test_cases.then: Card A's .card-body.scrollTop remains 0 (unchanged)

test_cases.title: Opening a 6th card results in 6 cards in DOM
test_cases.given: CardManager.openCards has 5 entries (5 cards open)
test_cases.when: CardManager.open('FR-006', artifactData, { x: 100, y: 100 }) is called
test_cases.then: CardManager.openCards.size === 6 and document.querySelectorAll('.artifact-card').length === 6

test_cases.title: Pan graph with 2 open cards — cards stay at fixed screen positions
test_cases.given: A graph is loaded with 2 open cards. Card A is at getBoundingClientRect().left = 400, top = 80
test_cases.when: The user drags the canvas from screen position (600, 400) to (400, 300) (a 200px left, 100px up pan)
test_cases.then: Card A's getBoundingClientRect().left is still 400 and top is still 80 (cards did not move with the graph)

test_cases.title: Scrolling mouse wheel over canvas zooms graph — cards stay at fixed positions
test_cases.given: A graph is loaded with 1 open card at getBoundingClientRect() = { left: 300, top: 100 }. cy.zoom() = 1.0
test_cases.when: The user scrolls the mouse wheel up 3 times over the canvas (not over the card)
test_cases.then: cy.zoom() > 1.0 (graph zoomed) AND the card's getBoundingClientRect().left = 300 and top = 100 (card did not move)

test_cases.title: Collapsing a node whose card is open leaves the card open
test_cases.given: A Feature node 'FR-002' has its card open (data-node-id='FR-002' exists in DOM) and its children are visible
test_cases.when: The user clicks node 'FR-002' (triggering collapse)
test_cases.then: The children are hidden (collapsed) AND document.querySelector('[data-node-id="FR-002"]') still exists in DOM (card remains open) — the toggle closes the card only on the second click (per FR-013 AC-013-02)

test_cases.title: cards.css uses position:fixed — cards are immune to canvas transform during pan/zoom
test_cases.given: css/cards.css is loaded
test_cases.when: Source is inspected
test_cases.then: .artifact-card has position:fixed so Cytoscape canvas transforms do not move cards

test_cases.title: Second card opens offset ≥20px from first card
test_cases.given: CardManager.openCards has 1 entry. CASCADE_OFFSET = 24. computePosition returns { left: 100, top: 80 } for the first card
test_cases.when: getCascadeCount() is called when openCards.size = 1, then computePosition is called for the second card
test_cases.then: The second card's computed position has left ≥ 124 or top ≥ 104 (offset ≥24px in at least one axis from the first card position)

test_cases.title: Third card is offset from second card, not from first
test_cases.given: CardManager.openCards has 2 entries. Card 1 at left=100, card 2 at left=124. CASCADE_OFFSET=24
test_cases.when: getCascadeCount() is called when openCards.size = 2
test_cases.then: getCascadeCount() returns 2, and the computed left for card 3 is 148 (100 + 2×24) — not 124 (which would be offset only from card 1)

test_cases.title: Cascade position wraps when overflow would occur
test_cases.given: window.innerWidth = 1280, CARD_WIDTH = 320, MARGIN = 8, CASCADE_OFFSET = 24. maxCascade = floor((1280 - 320 - 8 - 8) / 24) = 39. openCards.size = 40
test_cases.when: getCascadeCount() is called with openCards.size = 40
test_cases.then: getCascadeCount() returns 0 (40 % 40 = 0), causing the card to open at the initial anchor position (no overflow)

test_cases.title: E2E: PM clicks feature node, reads acceptance criteria list, closes card
test_cases.given: App running at http://localhost:3000. Demo project loaded. A Feature node with id 'FR-001' and acceptance_criteria: ['AC one', 'AC two', 'AC three'] is visible in the graph
test_cases.when: The user clicks the node 'FR-001', then reads the card, then clicks the X button
test_cases.then: (1) Card appears with data-node-id='FR-001' within 300ms. (2) Card body contains 3 <li> elements with text 'AC one', 'AC two', 'AC three'. (3) After clicking X, document.querySelector('[data-node-id="FR-001"]') returns null

test_cases.title: E2E: Developer opens 2 cards, pans graph, closes one — other card stays
test_cases.given: App running at http://localhost:3000. Demo project loaded with nodes 'FR-001' and 'FR-002' visible
test_cases.when: User clicks 'FR-001' (card A opens), clicks 'FR-002' (card B opens), drags canvas 100px left, then clicks X on card A
test_cases.then: (1) Both cards visible after clicks. (2) After pan: card A left position unchanged (fixed). (3) After closing card A: document.querySelector('[data-node-id="FR-001"]') = null, document.querySelector('[data-node-id="FR-002"]') exists
